obligations.reason: A visible zeroriscy_debug_unit and core debug/control modules indicate debug paths may reach protected CPU state; the check must determine whether authorization guards cover all debug operations rather than assuming debug logic is safe or unsafe.
obligations.subject: debug interface / debug unit
obligations.operation: read/write/override/trigger debug control of core state
obligations.object: core architectural state, debug control state, register file, PC/control-flow state
obligations.expected_guard: debug entry and debug operations should be mediated by explicit debug authorization/authentication or a trusted debug-mode state before protected core state is exposed or modified
state_conditions_to_check: debug request while core is running
required_evidence_slots: target core state or control object
reachable path from debug unit to object
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, path/reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated and does not list all debug ports; inspect RTL port connections and signal names to identify exact debug request and authorization signals.

obligations.reason: The scope contains a dedicated CSR register module with multiple decode cases; visible RTL should be checked for whether software CSR operations are permission-checked at decode and update sites.
obligations.subject: CPU software executing CSR instructions
obligations.object: control/status registers and security-relevant CPU state
obligations.expected_guard: CSR reads and writes that affect privilege/security state, exception/interrupt state, or debug state should be gated by legal CSR address, access type, current privilege/debug mode, and read-only/write permissions
obligations.candidate_path: zeroriscy_decoder/id_stage/cs_registers CSR decode and writeback path
obligations.uncertainty: Zero-riscy may implement a limited privilege model; determine expected guard from implemented CSR access attributes and mode signals, not from names alone.

obligations.reason: fc_demux instances route core instruction and data requests based on decode cases; permission analysis should ensure address routing preserves intended memory/peripheral access policy and does not create unguarded aliases.
obligations.subject: fabric controller core instruction/data bus master
obligations.object: SoC address regions, TCDM/peripheral access paths, instruction/data bus targets
obligations.expected_guard: address decode and security/privilege attributes should consistently restrict instruction and data accesses to protected regions or peripherals, with no alternate demux output bypassing the intended range or mode check
obligations.candidate_path: fc_subsystem core data/instruction buses -> fc_demux -> external/peripheral/TCDM buses
obligations.uncertainty: Protected regions are inferred from address-map and security-mode signals; inspect include definitions for concrete ranges and security attributes.

obligations.reason: A visible load/store unit generates data bus transactions; check whether it or downstream fabric enforces memory-region permissions for protected MMIO or memory regions.
obligations.subject: CPU load/store unit
obligations.object: data memory and memory-mapped protected regions
obligations.expected_guard: load and store requests to protected address regions should be mediated by address-range/PMP-like checks, privilege/security mode, and operation type before issuing bus requests
obligations.uncertainty: The presence of PMP is not established in the fact layer; if absent, document where protection is expected from SoC fabric rather than CPU.

obligations.reason: The interrupt controller exposes security-relevant outputs including core_irq_sec_o, fetch_en_o, and clock enable; visible RTL should show whether APB/configuration writes and event inputs are mediated by secure-mode or register protections.
obligations.subject: APB/MMIO software or event source
obligations.object: interrupt controller configuration, secure interrupt indication, fetch enable, core clock enable
obligations.expected_guard: writes or events that alter secure interrupt attribution, fetch enable, or core clocking should be gated by authorized APB access, secure mode/lifecycle state, or lock/protection bits
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults for fetch/clock/secure interrupt state
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer ports include security-relevant signals but not all APB port details due truncation; inspect full module to identify register map and write paths.

obligations.reason: Visible interfaces include test_mode_i, BIST, bypass, cache/private and clock-gating controls; these can be legitimate but require guards to prevent normal-mode access or state exposure through test paths.
obligations.subject: test/BIST/bypass interface
obligations.object: memory/test-visible state, cache/TCDM control state, core clock/fetch control
obligations.expected_guard: test, BIST, bypass, and clock-gate controls should only affect protected memories or core operation under authorized test/lifecycle mode and should not expose or modify memory contents in normal operation
obligations.candidate_path: test_mode_i/BIST/bypass/clkgate interfaces -> memories/cache/TCDM or core controls
obligations.uncertainty: Interfaces file defines signals but may not implement policy; inspect connected modules in this scope for actual mediation or note if policy is external.

obligations.reason: Controller and IF-stage FSMs plus debug and interrupt signals determine whether protected execution state is exposed or controlled across lifecycle transitions; check reset defaults and transitions for unsafe residual permissions.
obligations.subject: core control FSM, interrupt controller, debug unit
obligations.operation: reset/mode transition/trigger fetch or debug action
obligations.object: PC/fetch state, debug state, interrupt/fetch enable state
obligations.expected_guard: reset, halt, exception, interrupt, and debug mode transitions should clear or correctly gate stale protected state and should not leave debug/fetch controls enabled without authorization
obligations.candidate_path: zeroriscy_controller/if_stage/debug_unit reset and mode-transition control -> PC/fetch/debug state
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL inspection to identify exact FSM states and whether any outputs are externally observable or security-sensitive.

obligations.reason: The scope includes an fc_hwpe module and HW accelerator-style interfaces; permission obligations should check whether accelerator-originated transactions are routed and constrained like other bus masters.
obligations.subject: HWPE / hardware accelerator master
obligations.object: memory regions reachable through HWPE/fabric interfaces
obligations.expected_guard: hardware accelerator requests should be constrained by address-range/security policy and should not gain broader memory access than the CPU or configured permissions allow
obligations.candidate_path: fc_hwpe and hw accelerator interfaces -> core/fabric memory access
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not expose all fc_hwpe ports; inspect module to determine whether it is a bus master, wrapper, or control-only block.

evidence_slots.subject: APB/debug requester via apb_slave_debug and apb2per_debug_i
evidence_slots.operation: read/write debug registers; request GPR, CSR, and jump/PC debug operations
evidence_slots.object: debug settings/state, register file, CSR state, PC/jump control
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug entry and debug operations should be mediated by explicit debug authorization/authentication or a trusted debug-mode state before protected core state is exposed or modified
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: fc_subsystem connects apb_slave_debug through apb2per_debug_i directly to debug_req/debug_addr/debug_we/debug_wdata/debug_rdata. zeroriscy_debug_unit asserts debug_gnt_o for many accesses, including invalid accesses; debug registers at address class 6'b00_0000 are always accessible, while CSR/GPR/DNPC paths are guarded only by debug_halted_o. No auth/secure/lifecycle signal was observed in the debug unit ports or at the APB bridge.
evidence_slots.path: fc_subsystem.apb_slave_debug -> apb2per_debug_i -> debug_req/debug_addr/debug_we/debug_wdata -> core debug port -> zeroriscy_debug_unit -> settings_o/regfile_* / csr_* / jump_req_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: normal running debug request; halted/debug mode; reset initializes debug FSM/settings but does not add authorization
evidence_slots.impact: An APB-accessible requester able to reach apb_slave_debug can manipulate debug configuration and, once the core is halted, read/write GPRs/CSRs or redirect execution without a separate debug authorization decision, potentially compromising core control-flow and architectural state.

rtl_evidence.detail: apb2per_debug_i maps apb_slave_debug PADDR/PWDATA/PWRITE/PSEL/PENABLE to debug_req/debug_addr/debug_we/debug_wdata and returns debug_rdata.
core debug ports connected to debug_req/debug_addr/debug_we/debug_wdata/debug_rdata; debug_halt_i and debug_resume_i tied to 1'b0 in observed riscv_core instance.
debug interface has debug_req_i/debug_addr_i/debug_we_i/debug_wdata_i and outputs to settings, regfile, CSR, and jump control; no auth/secure port shown.
debug_gnt_o granted for debug register, GPR, CSR, and default classes; GPR/CSR/jump operation enables require debug_halted_o, but always-accessible debug register reads are selected for 6'b00_0000.
regfile, CSR, jump, and settings outputs are driven from debug unit state.
assertion warns on internal debug register access while not halted, but this is not a hardware authorization guard.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Exact reset/write logic for all always-accessible debug settings is partially truncated; exact SoC-level APB access policy to apb_slave_debug is not provided.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: The debug interface is memory-mapped through fc_subsystem apb2per_debug_i to zeroriscy core debug signals without an observed authentication/authorization guard; zeroriscy_debug_unit grants debug requests and allows always-accessible debug register reads/writes, while protected GPR/CSR/PC operations are only gated by debug_halted_o rather than by an authorization check at the APB/debug boundary.
inspection_records.notes: The halted-state guard partially protects GPR/CSR/PC access, but the expected debug authorization guard is absent in the observed path.

evidence_slots.subject: CPU software executing CSR instructions and debug CSR port
evidence_slots.object: mstatus, mtvec, mepc, mcause, mhartid, performance counter CSRs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: CSR reads and writes that affect privilege/security state, exception/interrupt state, or debug state should be gated by legal CSR address, access type, current privilege/debug mode, and read-only/write permissions
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: CSR module decodes fixed CSR addresses and updates mstatus/mepc/mcause/performance counters based on csr_op_i/csr_we_int/is_* signals. The observed port list includes csr_access_i, csr_addr_i, csr_op_i, exception save/restore inputs, but no current privilege input; comments indicate mstatus is always M-mode. Unknown/default CSR addresses read as zero/no update. No explicit illegal-access output was observed in the provided snippets.
evidence_slots.path: decoder/id_stage CSR instruction path and debug CSR port -> zeroriscy_cs_registers csr_access_i/csr_addr_i/csr_op_i -> CSR state registers
evidence_slots.state_condition: CSR write enable asserted; normal execution vs exception save/restore; performance counter CSR access
evidence_slots.impact: If untrusted or lower-privilege software can execute CSR instructions in this core configuration, it could alter interrupt enable, exception PC/cause, or performance counter state without a privilege/access-permission check; however the evidence also suggests a machine-mode-only implementation, so the permission boundary is not established.

rtl_evidence.detail: CSR register interface exposes csr_access_i/csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/csr_op_i/csr_rdata_o.
read logic returns mstatus, mtvec, mepc, mcause, mhartid; default does nothing.
write logic updates mstatus, mepc, mcause when csr_we_int is set; no privilege input or illegal access signal visible in snippet.
csr_op_i directly determines write/set/clear/none and csr_we_int.
performance counter CSRs 0x7A0/0x7A1/0x79F and 0x780-0x79F are selected and writable via CSR operations.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need decoder/id_stage evidence for illegal CSR instruction handling and whether any non-machine/user privilege mode exists. Need complete CSR module around access qualification and reset values.
inspection_records.notes: Evidence supports absence of a visible privilege guard in the CSR module, but not enough to classify as violation because zero-riscy may intentionally be M-mode only.

evidence_slots.subject: fabric-controller core instruction/data bus master
evidence_slots.object: TCDM/L2/SCM/peripheral address regions and demux outputs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: address decode and security/privilege attributes should consistently restrict instruction and data accesses to protected regions or peripherals, with no alternate demux output bypassing the intended range or mode check
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: fc_subsystem instantiates separate fc_demux blocks for instruction and data paths. fc_demux itself routes the slave request to master_port0 or master_port1 based on a single port_sel_i and handshake state; no address-range, privilege, or security-mode guard is present inside fc_demux. Address-map include files define multiple SoC/FC/APB/L2/SCM ranges and aliases, but the provided evidence does not show the derivation of port_sel_i or whether upstream address decode enforces the policy.
evidence_slots.path: core_instr_bus/core_data_bus -> fc_demux_instr_i/fc_demux_data_i -> l2_* or scm_l2_* master ports
evidence_slots.state_condition: instruction fetch vs data access; request/grant handshake; default/out-of-range address not established
evidence_slots.impact: If port_sel_i can select protected/alternate targets without a consistent address/security check, instruction or data transactions could be routed through an unintended alias or bypass path to protected memory/peripherals; the provided evidence does not prove such reachability.

rtl_evidence.detail: fc_demux has port_sel_i, one slave port, XBAR_TCDM master_port0, and UNICAD_MEM master_port1.
slave request/address/write signals are copied to internal paths; master_port1 grant is hardwired to 1'b1.
demux state captures destination on req_slave & gnt_slave and switches on port_sel_i.
static facts show core_data_bus/core_instr_bus and fc_demux_instr_i/fc_demux_data_i instances.
address map includes CLUSTER_DATA, SOC_L2, SCM aliases, SOC_APB, FC_PERIPH_APB, FC_APB, and STDOUT ranges.
CLUSTER_ALIAS and FC_ALIAS are defined.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full fc_subsystem lines deriving is_scm_instr_req/is_scm_data_req/port_sel_i and any upstream fabric security checks; protected regions/security attributes are not defined in the provided evidence.
inspection_records.notes: fc_demux is not itself a permission guard; classification depends on the missing address decode and SoC policy.

evidence_slots.subject: CPU load/store unit
evidence_slots.object: data memory and memory-mapped protected regions
evidence_slots.expected_guard: load and store requests to protected address regions should be mediated by address-range/PMP-like checks, privilege/security mode, and operation type before issuing bus requests
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: zeroriscy_load_store_unit exposes data_req_o, data_addr_o, data_we_o, byte enables, write data, read data, and error inputs. Observed LSU snippets show data alignment/misalignment and sign-extension behavior, not address-range/PMP/security checks. fc_subsystem connects core data bus signals to downstream demux/fabric; bus address ranges are defined in includes. No PMP-like module or privilege/security input was observed in the LSU snippets.
evidence_slots.path: EX stage address/data -> zeroriscy_load_store_unit -> data bus signals -> fc_subsystem core_data_bus -> fc_demux_data_i/fabric
evidence_slots.state_condition: load/store; misaligned/split transaction; error response input present but denial source unknown
evidence_slots.impact: If the downstream fabric does not enforce permissions, CPU stores/loads may be issued to any mapped MMIO or memory address generated by software, including protected regions; however the provided evidence does not establish the intended protected-region policy or whether downstream fabric denies accesses.

rtl_evidence.detail: LSU outputs data_req_o, data_addr_o, data_we_o, data_be_o, data_wdata_o and receives data_rdata_i/data_err_i.
EX stage provides data_we_ex_i, data_type_ex_i, data_req_ex_i, and adder_result_ex_i; LSU reports misalignment.
observed logic handles read alignment/sign extension, not permission checks.
state registers track misalignment and read data state.
core data interface connects data_addr/data_req/data_be/data_we/data_wdata/data_rvalid/data_err.
SoC memory/peripheral address ranges are defined.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full LSU request-generation logic and downstream fabric/error response policy. No PMP/security mode evidence was provided.
inspection_records.notes: The LSU appears to be a transaction generator/alignment unit rather than a permission enforcement point in the observed snippets.

evidence_slots.subject: APB/MMIO software and event sources
evidence_slots.object: interrupt mask/int/ack/FIFO state, core_irq_sec_o, fetch_en_o, core_clock_en_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: writes or events that alter secure interrupt attribution, fetch enable, or core clocking should be gated by authorized APB access, secure mode/lifecycle state, or lock/protection bits
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Module defines APB slave, event inputs, core_secure_mode_i, core_irq_sec_o, core_clock_en_o, and fetch_en_o. APB write/read are derived directly from psel/penable/pwrite. Read path returns r_int/r_ack/r_mask/r_fifo_event. pready is always 1 and pslverr always 0. core_clock_en_o and fetch_en_o are tied to 1'b1. No lock bit, secure-mode check, or APB authorization guard is visible in the provided snippets.
evidence_slots.path: fc_subsystem apb_slave_eu -> apb_interrupt_cntrl APB slave -> interrupt registers/events -> core_irq_req_o/core_irq_sec_o/fetch_en_o/core_clock_en_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: APB read/write; reset defaults not fully shown; core_secure_mode_i present but use not shown in snippets; fetch/clock outputs always enabled in observed code
evidence_slots.impact: Any requester able to access the APB interrupt controller can change interrupt mask/pending/ack state and observe FIFO/event status without an in-module authorization check; hardwired fetch/clock enables also prevent secure software from disabling core fetch/clock through this block, potentially undermining intended lifecycle or secure interrupt control.

rtl_evidence.detail: register map defines REG_MASK, REG_MASK_SET/CLEAR, REG_INT, REG_INT_SET/CLEAR, REG_ACK, REG_ACK_SET/CLEAR, and REG_FIFO.
ports include test_mode_i, event inputs, core_irq_sec_o, core_secure_mode_i, core_clock_en_o, fetch_en_o, and APB_BUS.Slave.
core_clock_en_o and fetch_en_o assigned 1'b1; APB write/read derived only from psel/penable/pwrite.
read path returns r_int/r_ack/r_mask/r_fifo_event on APB reads.
APB pready is 1'b1 and pslverr is 1'b0.
static facts show fc_eu_i instance of apb_interrupt_cntrl in fc_subsystem.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Write-update logic for r_mask/r_int/r_ack and exact core_irq_sec_o computation is partly truncated; SoC-level APB access restrictions outside this module are unknown.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: apb_interrupt_cntrl exposes interrupt mask/int/ack/FIFO registers over APB and drives security-relevant core_irq_sec_o plus fetch/clock outputs, but the observed register read/write decode is gated only by APB psel/penable/pwrite and address decode, with no secure-mode/lock authorization; fetch_en_o and core_clock_en_o are hardwired enabled.
inspection_records.notes: The violation is based on absence of an in-module secure/lock guard for APB-visible interrupt control and hardwired fetch/clock outputs.

evidence_slots.subject: test/BIST/bypass/clock-gate interface
evidence_slots.operation: test enable, bypass, flush, BIST configure/observe, clock-gate control
evidence_slots.object: memory/test-visible state, cache/TCDM control, core clock/fetch control, HWPE test mode
evidence_slots.expected_guard: test, BIST, bypass, and clock-gate controls should only affect protected memories or core operation under authorized test/lifecycle mode and should not expose or modify memory contents in normal operation
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Static facts and interface snippets show test_mode/test_en ports and CLKGATE_CONFIG_BUS, MMU_CONFIG_BUS, BIST_BUS, cache control, bypass, and flush signals. fc_subsystem passes test_en_i to riscv_core and fc_hwpe; apb_interrupt_cntrl has test_mode_i. The provided scope does not show implementation logic that uses these test/bypass/BIST controls or an authorization/lifecycle guard.
evidence_slots.path: test_en_i/test_mode_i and interface-defined BIST/bypass/clkgate signals -> core/HWPE/memory/control modules, exact connected enforcement path not shown
evidence_slots.state_condition: test_mode_i/test_en_i low vs high; BIST and bypass result phases not implemented in observed snippets
evidence_slots.impact: If these interfaces are driven in normal operation without lifecycle authorization, they could alter clocking, bypass cache/TCDM paths, initiate BIST, or expose test result state; however the observed evidence only proves signal/interface presence, not an unguarded active path in this scope.

rtl_evidence.detail: apb_interrupt_cntrl has test_mode_i input.
fc_subsystem has test_en_i input.
test_en_i is passed to the core instance.
test_en_i is passed as test_mode_i to fc_hwpe.
CLKGATE_CONFIG_BUS defines clkgate_tcdm/core/scm/int/hwacc/sel without guard logic.
MMU_CONFIG_BUS defines sequential-section configuration signals.
BIST_BUS defines bist_en, bist_done, bist_result, and bist_pattern.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need connected modules implementing BIST, bypass, cache/private, and clkgate policies. Interface definitions alone do not prove access or lack of guard.
inspection_records.notes: No candidate violation without an observed normal-mode control path or missing guard at a use site.

evidence_slots.subject: core control FSM, debug unit, interrupt controller
evidence_slots.operation: reset/mode transition/trigger fetch or debug action
evidence_slots.object: PC/fetch state, debug state, interrupt/fetch enable state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: reset, halt, exception, interrupt, and debug mode transitions should clear or correctly gate stale protected state and should not leave debug/fetch controls enabled without authorization
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: debug_unit reset initializes stall_cs to RUNNING, debug cause to HALT, single-step hit to 0, address/data/state/select/request flops to zero, settings_q to zero. apb_interrupt_cntrl hardwires fetch_en_o and core_clock_en_o to 1. Controller and IF-stage FSMs exist with fetch_enable_i and PC control, but their transition details are largely truncated. No complete evidence shows unsafe residual permissions across reset/exception/debug transitions.
evidence_slots.state_condition: reset deassertion; debug entry/exit; fetch enable changes; exception/return transitions partially observed
evidence_slots.impact: Unsafe reset or transition gating could leave debug/fetch controls enabled or stale control-flow state reachable after reset/halt/interrupt; the provided evidence shows some safe debug reset defaults but is insufficient to prove or disprove lifecycle permission failure.

rtl_evidence.detail: debug stall FSM reset sets stall_cs RUNNING, dbg_cause_q DBG_CAUSE_HALT, dbg_ssth_q 0.
debug interface flops reset addr/wdata/state/request/select signals and settings_q to zero; update on debug_req_i.
fetch_en_o and core_clock_en_o are tied high.
controller ports include fetch_enable_i, instr_req_o, pc_set_o, pc_mux_o, indicating fetch/PC transition control.
IF stage module exists with clock/reset; full FSM details not provided.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full controller/IF-stage state transition logic, interrupt acknowledge behavior, and reset assignments for APB interrupt registers/core_irq_sec_o.
inspection_records.notes: Debug reset evidence is not itself a violation; hardwired fetch/clock enable is covered more directly under PERM-APBINT-001.

evidence_slots.subject: HWPE / hardware accelerator master configured by APB software
evidence_slots.object: memory regions reachable via l2_hwpe_master / hwacc_xbar_master TCDM ports
evidence_slots.expected_guard: hardware accelerator requests should be constrained by address-range/security policy and should not gain broader memory access than the CPU or configured permissions allow
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: fc_subsystem instantiates fc_hwpe with APB slave hwpe config and l2_hwpe_master array. fc_hwpe converts APB config to periph_* signals via apb2per and instantiates mac_top_wrap, connecting tcdm_req/tcdm_add/tcdm_wen/tcdm_be/tcdm_wdata/tcdm_rdata. No range check, security mode, privilege attribute, or lock of configuration while transfers are active was observed in fc_hwpe snippets.
evidence_slots.path: fc_subsystem.apb_slave_hwpe -> fc_hwpe.apb2per -> mac_top_wrap config -> tcdm_* signals -> hwacc_xbar_master/l2_hwpe_master
evidence_slots.state_condition: APB configuration; accelerator active transfer; read vs write through tcdm_wen
evidence_slots.impact: An APB requester that can program HWPE may cause the accelerator to generate memory reads/writes on its master ports without an in-wrapper permission check, potentially bypassing CPU-side access controls or reaching memory regions not intended for accelerator access if the downstream fabric does not constrain it.

rtl_evidence.detail: fc_subsystem exposes l2_hwpe_master array and apb_slave_hwpe.
fc_hwpe instantiated with hwacc_xbar_master=l2_hwpe_master and hwacc_cfg_slave=apb_slave_hwpe.
fc_hwpe ports include test_mode_i, XBAR_TCDM_BUS.Master hwacc_xbar_master array, APB_BUS.Slave hwacc_cfg_slave, evt_o, busy_o.
internal accelerator request/address/write/byte-enable/data signals are declared for TCDM and peripheral config.
apb2per converts hwacc_cfg_slave APB signals into periph_req/periph_add/periph_we/periph_wdata/periph_be.
mac_top_wrap receives periph config and drives tcdm_req/tcdm_add/tcdm_wen/tcdm_be/tcdm_data.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The snippet is truncated before hwacc_xbar_master assignments and mac_top_wrap policy; downstream fabric address restrictions are unknown.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: fc_hwpe exposes an APB-configured hardware accelerator wrapper with TCDM master ports, but the observed wrapper passes APB configuration directly through apb2per to mac_top_wrap and connects accelerator TCDM requests to fabric master ports without an observed address-range/security/permission guard.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate depends on APB reachability and absence of downstream range enforcement; within fc_hwpe wrapper no guard was observed.
